content.plain_english: This week your child is meeting the very first ideas of multiplication and division. They are counting in 2s, 5s and 10s, and learning to make 'equal groups' — sets of the same size.
content.in_school: Children make small groups of the same size with cubes or counters — five plates with two grapes each, for example. They start to see 'five lots of two' and notice they can count in 2s to find the total. They share things equally between people: 8 apples between 2 children means 4 each.
content.home_activity: At snack time, share a small handful of grapes (say 10) equally between everyone at the table. How many each? Then arrange biscuits in a tray of 2 rows and 3 columns — count two threes or three twos and see they both make 6.
dinner_table_questions: If 4 children have 2 sweets each, how many sweets altogether?
Share these 10 grapes between 2 of us. How many each?
Count in 5s with me up to 30.

key_vocabulary.definition: Groups that all have the same number.
Split a number into equal parts so each person gets the same.
A picture of equal rows and columns — like rows of seats.

example_questions: If I have 3 plates and put 5 grapes on each, how many grapes are there?
Share these 12 sweets between 4 children. How many each?
content.times_table_tip: Year 1 children practise counting in 2s, 5s and 10s. Try counting in 2s while you walk to the front door — slow steps, slow numbers — then in 5s on the way back.
